topic.title: Working with Coding Agent
topic.p: This project explains how to prepare an application repository so a coding agent can participate in day-to-day development with the Vczh Libraries. The expected setup is copied from the Release repository: the .github folder, AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, and a project-specific Project.md. After these files are in place, an agent has local instructions, copied documentation, build scripts, debugging scripts, and task-log conventions.

The workflow is:
Install platform tools so build, run, and debug scripts can work.
Copy the agent context files from the Release repository into the application repository.
Write Project.md so the agent knows which solution, projects, generated files, and platform folders belong to the application.
Use prompt jobs such as investigate and refine for repeatable development work.
For GacUI applications, expose AutomationService and prepare basic native-window handling so the agent can debug UI behavior without depending on operating-system UI Automation.

The files copied from Release are starting points. They should be kept close to the application repository and changed when the application has different build steps, verification projects, generated files, or debugging expectations.
